DetectionRule.Name: UAC Bypass Technique Detection
DetectionRule.Description: Detects User Account Control (UAC) bypass techniques that allow standard users to elevate privileges to Administrator level without proper consent prompts. Identifies common bypass methods including DLL hijacking, auto-elevating COM objects, and registry modifications.

RemediationSteps.Step: Restore UAC settings to default (EnableLUA=1, PromptOnSecureDesktop=1)
Reset permissions on UAC-related registry keys
Check for any processes running with elevated privileges
Review event logs for privilege escalation attempts
Enable UAC and set appropriate security level
Implement least privilege and remove unnecessary admin rights

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized administrative changes during maintenance
Exclude system processes and legitimate admin tools
Time-based: Focus on non-business hours modifications
User-based: Require admin privileges for UAC modifications

DetectionRule.Name: Token Impersonation and Privilege Escalation
DetectionRule.Description: Detects token impersonation and manipulation techniques used to escalate privileges, including stealing access tokens, impersonating higher-privileged users, and bypassing access controls. Identifies API calls commonly used in token manipulation attacks.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1134.001 - Access Token Manipulation: Token Impersonation/Theft, T1134.002 - Access Token Manipulation: Create Process with Token

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Isolate affected device from network
Disable compromised accounts and reset passwords
Check for lateral movement and additional compromised systems
Preserve memory dumps for forensic analysis
Review and revoke any elevated privileges granted
Enable LSA Protection and Credential Guard

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate security tools and admin utilities
API-based: Focus on unusual combinations of token APIs

DetectionRule.Name: Vulnerable Driver Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
DetectionRule.Description: Detects exploitation of vulnerable kernel drivers for privilege escalation, including known vulnerable drivers (BYOVD - Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) and unusual driver loading patterns. Identifies malicious driver installation and exploitation attempts.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1547.008 - Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: LSASS Driver, T1068 - Exploitation for Privilege Escalation

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Isolate affected device and disable vulnerable driver
Remove unauthorized drivers using pnputil or Device Manager
Enable Kernel DMA Protection and Secure Boot
Check for exploitation signs (token theft, privilege escalation)
Update or remove vulnerable driver from all systems
Implement Driver Block Rules and HVCI

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate driver updates and installations
Time-based: Focus on unexpected driver installations
Path-based: Allow drivers from trusted directories only
